tool_args.label: Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevard (Q52/53) Public Design Workshop

payload.title: Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevard (Q52/53) Public Design Workshop

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Proposed SBS route on Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards based on the Q52/53 LTD bus route, from Woodside to the Rockaways. Includes main road bus lanes, median stations, offset bus lanes on Cross Bay Blvd, off-board fare collection, and other transit priority treatments.

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

tool_args.label: NYC Department of Design and Construction (NYCDDC)

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Design and Construction

payload.description: Proposed SBS route along Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards based on the Q52 LTD route
Proposed SBS route along Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards based on the Q53 LTD route
Local bus route along the Woodhaven/Cross Bay corridor
Local bus route along the Woodhaven/Cross Bay corridor
Existing limited bus route that SBS is based on
Existing limited bus route that SBS is based on
Main road bus lanes; all buses use median stations. Proposed cross-section includes 12' bus lanes, medians with trees, and travel lanes.
Offset bus lanes; SBS buses stop at bus bulbs; Local buses stop at the curb. Two design options presented with different lane configurations.

payload.limits: 14 miles from Woodside to the Rockaways
payload.description: Total SBS corridor spanning Woodhaven Blvd and Cross Bay Blvd. 30,000+ daily bus riders. 400,000 residents within 15-min walk. Vision Zero Priority Corridor since 2009.

tool_args.label: Main road bus lanes on Woodhaven Blvd

payload.description: Proposed 12' bus lanes in main road, with median stations. All buses use main road bus lanes and median stations.

tool_args.label: Median bus stations on Woodhaven Blvd

payload.description: High-quality median bus stations for all buses (SBS, Local, and Express). Stations include boarding areas for Local/Express and SBS separated by a maneuvering space, with fencing/screen, and station access from intersection crosswalks or mid-block.

tool_args.label: Offset bus lanes on Cross Bay Blvd

payload.description: Offset bus lanes. SBS buses stop at bus bulbs; Local/express buses stop at the curb. Maintains parking/deliveries at the curb. Option 1: 2 travel lanes each direction with left-turn bays. Option 2: 3 travel lanes each direction with shared left-turn lanes.

tool_args.label: Bus bulb stations on Cross Bay Blvd

payload.description: The sidewalk is extended to meet the bus lane. SBS buses stop at the bus bulb; Local/Express buses stop at the curb. SBS buses can pass Local and Express buses.

tool_args.label: Off-board fare collection for Q52/53 SBS

payload.description: Fare machines at every SBS stop. Pay with Metrocard or coins. Customers can board at any door.

tool_args.label: Over 6 miles of bus lanes on the corridor

payload.description: Over 6 miles of bus lanes. Opportunity to explore unique treatments along Woodhaven Boulevard including physical separation (hard barriers, soft barriers/rumble strips) and bus lane materials.
Left-turn bays at selected locations on Woodhaven Blvd. Slip lanes at select locations allow vehicles to move between service road and main road.

tool_args.label: Public Design Workshop - April 29, 2015 (Cross Bay Blvd)

payload.event_name: Public Design Workshop - Cross Bay Blvd

payload.description: Capital project construction (2017-2018) → SERVICE STARTS

payload.claim_text: Vision Zero Priority Corridor Since 2009 on Woodhaven & Cross Bay Blvds
payload.description: Corridor designated as Vision Zero Priority Corridor since 2009
payload.subject: Woodhaven & Cross Bay Blvds

payload.description: Q52 extension under consideration, noted on the SBS route map
payload.subject: Q52 SBS

tool_args.label: Chosen concept: Main road bus lanes

payload.claim_text: Concept 2 Main Road Bus Lanes: Substantial transit improvement. Most potential for pedestrian and safety improvements. Balances local vehicle access and thru vehicle traffic.
payload.description: Concept 2 selected as the preferred corridor design after community input and technical analysis

tool_args.label: Cross Bay Blvd Option 2 currently shown

payload.claim_text: Based on feedback from the Community Advisory Committee, Option 2 is currently shown in the design plans; however, Option 1 will also be analyzed in terms of safety and traffic
payload.subject: Cross Bay Boulevard design

tool_args.label: Community feedback: bus reliability, safety concerns

payload.claim_text: Community feedback summary: 1. Bus service is unreliable and slow during rush hour 2. Transit improvements needed especially in Rockaways 3. Pedestrian crossings are long and dangerous 4. Congestion leads to long and difficult trips 5. Changing road widths and configurations make corridor difficult to navigate

payload.scope: Woodhaven / Cross Bay SBS corridor from Woodside to the Rockaways
